html:
Exclusive to:

Webber reviving.

Webber's Skull is an item used to unlock the Reign of Giants Character, Webber. It has a 5% chance to drop when any Spider dies, including variants in Caves and Ruins. It can also be dropped when any tier Spider Den or Spilagmite is destroyed or a Spider Queen is killed with the same chance. The skull will not drop if it already exists in the world or if Webber is already unlocked. Trapped Spiders will not drop the skull.

Burying Webber's Skull in a dug-up Grave will cause Webber to appear under a "spotlight" after the grave is struck by Lightning*. He will then jump back down into the grave, disappearing, while 6 Spiders appear in his place. Webber is then unlocked in the character menu. The Spiders will be aggressive towards the player, though killing them is not necessary for unlocking Webber.

Webber's Skull will keep dropping as long as it hasn't been buried, but not more than one in each world unless the previous one is destroyed.

*After being buried, the grave will be struck immediately

Tips

  • Increasing the amount of Spiders and Pigs in World Customization can help with finding the Skull, since Spiders fighting with Pigs will be more commonplace, and each Spider killed by Pigs has a chance to drop the Skull as well. However, players would have to pay attention to all the battles around, since the skull isn't going to drop more than once per world.

Trivia

  • The addition of Webber's Skull makes Webber one of six playable characters that is not unlocked via experience, the other five being Wes, Maxwell, Woodlegs, Wilbur and Wilba.
  • Webber's Skull will become "Ashes of Webber's Skull" (Ashes) when carried through the Teleportato.
  • Many other characters have skulls found in the game files but they are unused.
  • While the skull does not drop in Don't Starve Together, all DST-exclusive characters except Wanda have quotes for it in their speech files.
plainText:
Exclusive to:
Webber's Skull
Does not stack
Perk
Unlocks Webber as a playable character.
Dropped by
"webberskull"
“Poor little guy. He deserves a proper funeral.”
–Wilson
“It's not myyy way, but a burial will have to do.”
–Willow
“I crush skull!”
–Wolfgang
“He's better this way.”
–Wendy
“ANALYSIS SHOWS 50% SPIDER AND 50% HUMAN”
–WX-78
“That is a most unusual skull.”
–Wickerbottom
“The skull gives me the willies, too.”
–Woodie
“Don't think you'll be able to escape that easily.”
–Maxwell
“Wonderful! The skull of an arachnid humanoid hybrid.”
–Wagstaff
“He föught böldly, but his burial will nöt be that öf a Viking”
–Wigfrid
“How did this get here?”
–Webber
“Not your run of the mill skull.”
–Walani
“Stop staring at me or I'll bury you!”
–Warly
“Whut sorta neck did this sit atop?”
–Woodlegs
“ALAS, POOR SPIDER”
–Wilba
“Oh. Oh dear”
–Wormwood
“I should bury it before it starts haunting me.”
–Wheeler
“I swear the kid'd lose his head if it weren't... wait.”
–Winona
“Hm. There's two souls wedged inside.”
–Wortox
“That not where bone belong!”
–Wurt
“No Woby, these bones aren't for chewing on!”
–Walter
Webber reviving.
Webber's Skull is an item used to unlock the Reign of Giants Character, Webber. It has a 5% chance to drop when any Spider dies, including variants in Caves and Ruins. He will then jump back down into the grave, disappearing, while 6 Spiders appear in his place. Webber is then unlocked in the character menu. The Spiders will be aggressive towards the player, though killing them is not necessary for unlocking Webber.
Webber's Skull will keep dropping as long as it hasn't been buried, but not more than one in each world unless the previous one is destroyed.
*After being buried, the grave will be struck immediately
Tips[edit | edit source]
Increasing the amount of Spiders and Pigs in World Customization can help with finding the Skull, since Spiders fighting with Pigs will be more commonplace, and each Spider killed by Pigs has a chance to drop the Skull as well. However, players would have to pay attention to all the battles around, since the skull isn't going to drop more than once per world.

The addition of Webber's Skull makes Webber one of six playable characters that is not unlocked via experience, the other five being Wes, Maxwell, Woodlegs, Wilbur and Wilba.

[[File:Webber Revival.png|thumb|Webber reviving.]]
'''Webber's Skull''' is an item used to unlock the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' [[Character]], [[Webber]]. It has a 5% chance to drop when any [[Spider]] dies, including variants in [[Caves]] and [[Ruins]]. It can also be dropped when any tier [[Spider Den]] or [[Spilagmite]] is destroyed or a [[Spider Queen]] is killed with the same chance. The skull will not drop if it already exists in the world or if Webber is already unlocked. [[Trap]]ped Spiders will not drop the skull.

Burying Webber's Skull in a dug-up [[Grave]] will cause Webber to appear under a "spotlight" after the grave is struck by [[Lightning]]*. He will then jump back down into the grave, disappearing, while 6 Spiders appear in his place. Webber is then unlocked in the character menu. The Spiders will be aggressive towards the player, though killing them is not necessary for unlocking Webber.

Webber's Skull will keep dropping as long as it hasn't been buried, but not more than one in each world unless the previous one is destroyed.

<nowiki>*</nowiki>After being buried, the grave will be struck immediately 

== {{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Increasing the amount of Spiders and [[Pigs]] in [[World Customization]] can help with finding the Skull, since Spiders fighting with Pigs will be more commonplace, and each Spider killed by Pigs has a chance to drop the Skull as well. However, players would have to pay attention to all the battles around, since the skull isn't going to drop more than once per world.

== {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The addition of Webber's Skull makes Webber one of six playable characters that is not unlocked via experience, the other five being [[Wes]], [[Maxwell (character)|Maxwell]], [[Woodlegs]], [[Wilbur]] and [[Wilba]].
* Webber's Skull will become "Ashes of Webber's Skull" ([[Ashes]]) when carried through the [[Wooden Thing|Teleportato]].
* Many other characters have skulls found in the game files but they are unused.
* While the skull does not drop in ''Don't Starve Together'', all DST-exclusive characters except [[Wanda]] have quotes for it in their speech files.
